examples.title: Speed = Distance ÷ Time
examples.content: Speed combines length and time: meters ÷ seconds = m/s. This is a derived unit. You create it by combining base units.

examples.title: Force = Mass × Acceleration
examples.content: Force uses kg × m/s² = kg·m/s². This combo is so common it gets a special name: Newton (N). But it's really just a combination of base units.

examples.title: Energy = Force × Distance
examples.content: Energy = (kg·m/s²) × m = kg·m²/s². This is the Joule (J). Again, just base units combined.
